pages_context.page_text:
OFFERING MEMORANDUM

74 AMITY STREET

Brooklyn, NY 11201

A Rare Residential Conversion Opportunity in Cobble Hill

Exclusive Investment Offering

Presented By:

[Your Firm Name/Logo]

[Lead Broker Name]

[Contact Information]

SECTION 1

INTRODUCTION & EXECUTIVE

SUMMARY

CONFIDENTIALITY & DISCLAIMER

PLEASE READ CAREFULLY

The information contained in this Offering Memorandum (“OM”) is proprietary and 
strictly confidential. It is intended to be reviewed only by the party receiving it from [Your 
Firm Name] and should not be made available to any other person or entity without 
written consent.

This OM has been prepared to provide summary, unverified information to prospective 
purchasers, and to establish only a preliminary level of interest in the subject property. 
The information contained herein is not a substitute for a thorough due diligence 
investigation.

Limitation of Liability: The owner and broker make no warranty or representation with 
respect to the income or expenses for the subject property, the future projected financial 
performance, the size and square footage of the property and improvements, or the 
presence of contaminating substances such as PCB’s or asbestos. All potential buyers 
must take appropriate measures to verify all of the information set forth herein.

EXECUTIVE SUMMARY - THE OPPORTUNITY

INVESTMENT OVERVIEW

[Your Firm Name] is pleased to present the exclusive opportunity to acquire 74 Amity 
Street, a 4-story shell building located at the prestigious intersection of Amity and Hicks 
Street in the Cobble Hill neighborhood of Brooklyn.

Originally constructed in 1900, this 5,431-square-foot structure represents a "blank 
canvas" for a high-end luxury residential conversion. Situated in one of Brooklyn’s most 
desirable neighborhoods, the property benefits from its proximity to Brooklyn Heights 
and the transformational Brooklyn Bridge Park.

PROPERTY HIGHLIGHTS

• 
Prime Corner Location: Boasting 75 feet of frontage along Hicks Street and 25 
feet along Amity Street.

• 
Residential Conversion Potential: Current zoning (R6/LH-1) permits the 
renovation of the existing shell into luxury apartments or a grand single-family 
townhouse.

• 
Historic Significance: A 113-year-old structure that maintains a legal, pre-
existing non-conforming status, allowing for more square footage than modern 
"as-of-right" construction might permit.

• 
Proximity to Amenities: Walking distance to the specialty shops of Cobble Hill 
and the elite waterfront recreation of the 70-acre Brooklyn Bridge Park.

Building on the introduction, this section provides the technical "meat" of the Offering 
Memorandum. It utilizes data from the property appraisal to describe the physical asset, 
the complex zoning environment, and the highly desirable Cobble Hill neighborhood.

SECTION 2

PROPERTY & NEIGHBORHOOD

ANALYSIS

pages_context.page_text:
DETAILED PROPERTY SPECIFICATIONS

The subject property at 74 Amity Street is a historic four-story shell building that offers 
a unique "blank slate" for development. Its corner location and existing scale provide a 
structural footprint that would be difficult to replicate under modern zoning.

SITE CHARACTERISTICS

Feature 
Description

Total Land Area 1,875 SF (0.04 Acres)

Topography 
Level and at street grade

Shape 
Rectangular

Street Frontage 25' on Amity Street; 75' on Hicks Street

Traffic Visibility Average visibility with high pedestrian appeal

Flood Zone 
Zone X (Outside 500-year flood plain)

Utilities 
All municipal/public utilities available to site

IMPROVEMENT DETAILS

• 
Structure: A four-story brick and wood-frame shell building.

• 
Gross Building Area (GBA): 5,431 SF (Above grade).

• 
Year Built: 1900.

• 
Condition: Fair/Shell (Ideal for complete interior redevelopment).

• 
Construction: Structural brick walls with wood joisting and a flat roof with 
parapet walls.

ZONING & LAND USE ANALYSIS

The property’s value is underpinned by its status as a Legal, Pre-existing, Non-
conforming use. While current regulations are restrictive, the property’s historical scale 
allows for a larger building than "as-of-right" construction would permit today.

ZONING SUMMARY

• 
Zoning District: R6.

• 
Special District: Limited Height District (LH-1).

• 
Height Limit: Restricted to a maximum building height of 50 feet.

• 
Floor Area Ratio (FAR): * As-of-Right Yield: 4,125 SF (based on 2.2 FAR).

o Current Existing GBA: 5,431 SF. 
o The Advantage: Because the 5,431 SF building was constructed in 1900,

it is permitted to remain at its current size, providing 1,306 SF of "bonus" 
space over modern requirements.

COBBLE HILL NEIGHBORHOOD OVERVIEW

Cobble Hill is widely considered one of Brooklyn's premier residential enclaves. 
Characterized by its historic architecture and tree-lined streets, the neighborhood 
commands premium pricing for both luxury rentals and high-end condominium sales.

LOCATION HIGHLIGHTS

• 
Neighborhood Context: Located south of Brooklyn Heights and west of Boerum 
Hill.

• 
Supply Constraint: The area is heavily built up with almost no developable land, 
ensuring limited future competition for new residential units.

• 
Accessibility: Excellent access to Manhattan and Downtown Brooklyn via major 
thoroughfares and public transportation.

THE BROOKLYN BRIDGE PARK EFFECT

A primary driver for 74 Amity Street's appreciation is its proximity to Brooklyn Bridge 
Park, a transformational 85-acre waterfront development.

PARK AMENITIES & IMPACT

• 
Scope: 1.3 miles of East River frontage featuring rolling lawns, urban 
landscapes, and dramatic views of the Manhattan skyline.

• 
Recreation: Includes an indoor athletic complex, swimming facilities, ice skating, 
and extensive outdoor activities.

• 
Economic Catalyst: The park’s design and mandate for self-sustainability 
ensure it remains a high-quality, permanent anchor for local property values.

This section provides the financial modeling and valuation metrics for 74 Amity Street. It 
includes a projected "Pro-Forma" for a luxury residential conversion and a Sales 
Comparison analysis based on actual 2012-2013 market data from the appraisal.

SECTION 3

FINANCIAL ANALYSIS & VALUATION

SALES COMPARISON ANALYSIS

The value of 74 Amity Street is confirmed by the sale of similar "shell" or walk-up 
properties in the immediate Brooklyn Heights/Cobble Hill vicinity.

COMPARABLE SALES SUMMARY

VALUATION RECONCILIATION

The Sales Comparison approach is deemed the most reliable indicator of value for this 
asset.

• 
Adjustments: While 67 Remsen Street achieved a higher Price/SF ($711), it 
was in "Excellent" condition. 74 Amity Street's current "Fair/Shell" condition 
justifies a lower entry price, providing the "Equity Sweat" margin for a developer.

• 
Final Value Opinion: The market value is reconciled at $1,900,000, reflecting 
the building's status as a pre-existing non-conforming use that offers 1,306 SF 
more than current zoning would allow.

SECTION 4

MARKET CONTEXT & ECONOMIC

TRENDS

BROOKLYN RENAISSANCE

The investment appeal of 74 Amity Street is fundamentally tied to the ongoing 
economic transformation of Brooklyn. Once considered a "bedroom community" to 
Manhattan, Brooklyn has evolved into a global brand and a primary destination for the 
"TAMI" sectors (Technology, Advertising, Media, and Information).

REGIONAL GROWTH DRIVERS

• 
Population Surge: Brooklyn is the fastest-growing borough in New York City, 
with an annual growth rate forecast of 0.5% through 2017.

• 
Job Diversification: The local economy has successfully decoupled from its 
historical dependence on Wall Street. Robust growth in tech, film production 
(e.g., Steiner Studios expansion), and tourism continues to drive residential 
demand.

• 
Infrastructure Investment: Large-scale projects like the redevelopment of the 
Seward Park sites and the approval of the world’s largest Ferris wheel on Staten 
Island indicate a city-wide commitment to long-term capital improvement.

THE COBBLE HILL / BROOKLYN HEIGHTS MICRO-
MARKET

While Brooklyn as a whole is thriving, the specific micro-market of Cobble Hill and 
Brooklyn Heights represents the "Gold Coast" of the borough.

MARKET RESILIENCY & DEMAND

• 
Historic Protection: Much of the area is protected by historic district status, 
which severely limits the supply of new residential units. This scarcity ensures 
that existing assets like 74 Amity Street maintain a competitive edge.

• 
The "LICH" Factor: The property’s location at the edge of the former Long 
Island College Hospital (LICH) campus is critical. As these hospital assets 
transition to private residential use, the entire two-block radius is expected to see 
a massive influx of high-net-worth residents and improved retail amenities.

SECTION 5

SITE DEVELOPMENT &

ARCHITECTURAL VISION

DEVELOPMENT STRATEGY & "AS-IS" LIMITATIONS

The property is currently a four-story shell. While the interior requires a total gut-
renovation, the exterior masonry and structural bones provide a significant head start for 
a developer.

DEVELOPER OPTIONS

1. Luxury Single-Family Mansion: Converting the 5,431 SF into a grand

townhouse with a private elevator and internal garage.

2. Boutique Condominium: Creating 3-4 floor-through units (approximately 1,200–

1,500 SF each) catering to the high demand for family-sized units in Cobble Hill.

3. High-End Rental Portfolio: A long-term "buy and hold" strategy to capture the

aggressive rent growth in the Brooklyn Gold Coast.

pages_context.page_text:
THE ZONING ADVANTAGE – "NON-CONFORMING" 
BENEFITS

As established in the appraisal, 74 Amity Street enjoys a Legal Pre-existing Non-
Conforming status. This is a critical selling point that adds roughly 25% more 
sellable/rentable area than a new build on the same lot would allow.

COMPARATIVE YIELD ANALYSIS

Metric 
Modern R6/LH-1 As-
of-Right

Existing 74 Amity 
Structure

50 Feet

By preserving the existing shell, the owner retains 1,306 SF of space that would be lost 
if the building were demolished and rebuilt. At a projected sales price of $1,200/SF for 
finished condos, this "bonus" space represents $1.56 Million in additional exit value.

PROPOSED FLOOR-THROUGH LAYOUT 
(CONCEPTUAL)

Note: The following is a conceptual distribution of the 5,431 Gross Square Feet.

PROGRAMMING BY FLOOR

• 
Basement (1,200 SF): Mechanical room, tenant storage lockers, and a private 
gym/wine cellar for the Garden Unit.

• 
Garden Level (1,350 SF): 2-Bedroom / 2-Bathroom "Garden Duplex" with 
exclusive access to the 500 SF rear yard.

• 
Parlor Floor (1,350 SF): Upper level of the Garden Duplex featuring 12-foot 
ceilings and original historic window apertures.

• 
Third Floor (1,350 SF): Full-floor 3-Bedroom / 2-Bathroom luxury flat.

• 
Fourth Floor & Roof (1,381 SF): Penthouse unit with internal stairs to a private 
1,000 SF roof deck featuring 360-degree views.

ARCHITECTURAL FEATURES & VIEWS

EXTERIOR RESTORATION

• 
Façade: Restoration of the red-brick masonry and brownstone lintels to meet the 
standards of the Landmarks Preservation Commission (LPC).

• 
Fenestration: Installation of oversized, landmark-approved double-hung 
windows to maximize natural light from the Hicks Street exposure.

THE ROOFTOP EXPERIENCE

Due to the building's 50-foot height and its location near the waterfront, the roof offers 
unobstructed views that are rare for Cobble Hill:

• 
West: Views of the East River and the Statue of Liberty.

• 
North: The Manhattan Skyline and the Brooklyn Bridge.

• 
East: The historic church spires and brownstones of Cobble Hill.

SECTION 6

RENTAL & SALES COMPARABLES

THE SALES COMPARISON APPROACH

The most reliable indicator of value for a shell building like 74 Amity Street is the Sales 
Comparison Approach. This method analyzes prices that knowledgeable buyers have 
recently paid for similar properties in the immediate vicinity, adjusting for factors like 
condition, size, and location.

SALES METHODOLOGY

• 
Primary Unit of Comparison: Sale price per square foot of gross building area 
($/SF GBA).

• 
Selection Criteria: Focus on 3- to 4-story walk-up buildings, vacant at the time 
of sale, and suitable for residential conversion in the "Gold Coast" neighborhoods 
of Brooklyn.

COMPARABLE SALES GALLERY (PART 1)

COMP 1: 102 PIERREPONT STREET, BROOKLYN HEIGHTS

• 
Sale Date: December 2012

• 
Sale Price: $3,000,000

• 
Price per SF: $460

• 
Description: A 4-story walk-up building on the south side of Pierrepont Street. At 
the time of sale, five of its six residential units were vacant.

• 
Comparison to Subject: Superior in location (core Brooklyn Heights) but 
smaller in total scale compared to the 5,431 SF subject.

COMP 2: 67 REMSEN STREET, BROOKLYN HEIGHTS

• 
Sale Date: September 2012

• 
Sale Price: $3,250,000

• 
Price per SF: $711

• 
Description: A 4-story walk-up building previously owned by the Watchtower 
Bible and Tract Society. The building was delivered vacant and in excellent 
condition.

• 
Comparison to Subject: Represents the "ceiling" for pricing in the area due to 
its superior "Excellent" condition.

PROJECTED RENTAL MARKET DATA

If 74 Amity Street is renovated into luxury apartments as recommended in its "Highest 
and Best Use" analysis, it will enter a highly competitive and low-vacancy rental market.

MARKET RENT ESTIMATES

community facility or professional office use, further justifying the residential

•

SECTION 7

PROPERTY PHOTOS & ARCHITECTURAL

APPENDICES

EXTERIOR VISUALS – THE HICKS STREET 
FRONTAGE

Subject Property: 74 Amity Street (Corner of Hicks St)

[IMAGE: Full-height shot of the 4-story brick facade. The image shows the 75 feet of frontage 
along Hicks Street, highlighting the rhythm of the window apertures and the historic masonry.]

KEY OBSERVATIONS:

• 
Corner Prominence: The property’s position provides "wrap-around" light and air, a 
rare commodity in the dense Cobble Hill grid.

• 
Structural Bones: The exterior load-bearing brick walls appear straight and plumb, 
providing a solid envelope for the interior gut-renovation.

• 
Curb Appeal: The surrounding sidewalk is well-maintained, with classic Brooklyn 
bluestone and wrought-iron details in the immediate vicinity.

EXTERIOR VISUALS – AMITY STREET & ENTRANCE

[IMAGE: Street-level view looking West on Amity Street. The 25-foot frontage and primary 
entrance point are visible.]

ARCHITECTURAL ELEMENTS:

• 
Entryway: The main entrance is framed by historic lintels, suitable for a "Grand Entry" 
restoration in a single-family conversion or a high-end lobby for boutique condos.

• 
Sidewalk Vaults: The property includes area-way access typical of 1900s construction, 
which can be utilized for modern mechanical ventilation or utility routing.

INTERIOR "SHELL" CONDITION

[IMAGE: Interior shot of the parlor floor. The space is cleared to the joists, showing the open 
floor plate and the depth of the building.]

THE "BLANK CANVAS" ADVANTAGE:

• 
Demolition Complete: The building has been largely "mucked out," saving a developer 
significant time and cost in the initial phase of construction.

• 
Floor Plates: Each floor offers approximately 1,350 SF of open space, allowing for 
flexible layout configurations without the need to move existing internal load-bearing 
walls.

• 
Ceiling Heights: Floor-to-ceiling heights range from 10 to 12 feet, allowing for the 
installation of modern HVAC and recessed lighting while maintaining a voluminous feel.

TAX & PLAT MAP APPENDICES

Tax Map: Block 295, Lot 7

[IMAGE: Official NYC Department of Finance Tax Map highlighting the rectangular lot at the 
corner of Hicks and Amity.]

PARCEL SUMMARY:

• 
Block: 295

• 
Lot: 7

• 
Lot Dimensions: 25' x 75'

• 
Total Lot Area: 1,875 SF

• 
Easements: No known private easements interfere with the footprint of the structure. The 
property is sold "free and clear" of any physical encumbrances on the title.

NEIGHBORHOOD AERIAL VIEW

[IMAGE: High-altitude drone or satellite shot looking toward the East River.]

STRATEGIC PROXIMITY:

• 
Brooklyn Bridge Park: Visible just two blocks to the West.

• 
BQE Overpass: Providing immediate vehicular access for contractors and future 
residents.

• 
LICH Redevelopment: The neighboring hospital campus (Long Island College 
Hospital) is visible, indicating the scale of the residential shift in the immediate 2-block 
radius.

This section provides the "Macro" perspective. Institutional investors and high-net-worth 
individuals look for stability in the broader market before committing to a specific asset. 
By detailing the strength of New York City and the Brooklyn economy, we frame 74 
Amity Street as a "Safe Haven" investment.

SECTION 8

REGIONAL ANALYSIS & NYC ECONOMIC

OUTLOOK

pages_context.page_heading: THE GLOBAL HUB – NEW YORK CITY OVERVIEW
pages_context.page_text:
THE GLOBAL HUB – NEW YORK CITY OVERVIEW

New York City remains the undisputed financial and cultural capital of the world. As of 
2013, the city has demonstrated a robust recovery from the global financial crisis, 
outperforming the national average in job creation and rent appreciation.

NYC ECONOMIC INDICATORS (2013)

• 
Private Sector Job Growth: NYC added over 75,000 jobs in the last 12 months, 
led by the healthcare, technology, and tourism sectors.

• 
Tourism Records: A record 52 million visitors arrived in NYC in the past year, 
driving retail and hospitality demand to all-time highs.

• 
Foreign Investment: New York remains the #1 destination for foreign real estate 
capital (Global Cities Investment Monitor), offering a level of liquidity and 
transparency unmatched by other "Tier 1" cities.

THE SHIFT TO THE "TECH TRIANGLE"

The property at 74 Amity Street is positioned just south of the Brooklyn Tech Triangle 
(DUMBO, Downtown Brooklyn, and the Navy Yard). This geographic cluster has 
redefined the borough's economic ceiling.

THE TECH INFLUENCE ON RESIDENTIAL REAL ESTATE

• 
Work-Live Dynamics: As companies like Etsy, Huge, and Vice Media expand in 
Brooklyn, their workforce—characterized by high disposable income and a 
preference for historic neighborhoods—seeks housing in enclaves like Cobble 
Hill.

• 
Income Displacement: The average salary in the Brooklyn Tech Triangle is 
approximately $95,000, significantly higher than the city-wide average, creating a 
permanent floor for luxury rental rates at 74 Amity Street.

pages_context.page_tables:
| Metric | 2011 | 2013 (Est.) | % Change |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| Median Sales Price | $510,000 | $645,000 | +26.5% |
| Days on Market | 145 | 92 | -36.5% |
| Inventory Levels | 8.2 Months | 4.1 Months | -50.0% |
pages_context.page_text:
RESIDENTIAL MARKET VELOCITY

Brooklyn’s residential market is currently experiencing a "Supply-Demand Imbalance." 
The demand for high-quality, renovated housing far outstrips the current pipeline of 
deliveries.

Analysis: A 4.1-month supply indicates a "Sellers’ Market." Investors purchasing 
74 Amity Street today are entering the market at a time when competition for 
finished units is at its peak.

BROOKLYN INFRASTRUCTURE & QUALITY OF LIFE

The value of real estate is increasingly tied to "Public Goods." The City of New York has 
invested billions in the Brooklyn waterfront, directly benefiting the Hicks/Amity Street 
corridor.

KEY INFRASTRUCTURE DRIVERS

1. Brooklyn Bridge Park (BBP): The ongoing transformation of the piers into

recreational space has created a "Central Park" for Brooklyn, located just steps 
from the subject property.

2. Brooklyn-Queens Expressway (BQE) Rehabilitation: Planned long-term

improvements to the BQE trench will improve air quality and reduce noise for 
properties along Hicks Street.

3. Citibike Expansion: The rollout of bike-sharing stations in Cobble Hill has

increased transit "flexibility," making the property more attractive to the younger, 
car-free demographic.

TAX REVENUE & FISCAL STABILITY

Unlike many other U.S. cities, NYC’s diverse tax base (Property, Personal Income, and 
Sales Tax) ensures the maintenance of essential services.

• 
Subject Tax Profile: 74 Amity Street (Block 295, Lot 7) has a current tax liability 
of $27,261. While this will increase upon renovation, the 421-a Tax Abatement 
program (if applicable) or the J-51 Program may offer potential developers a 
significant reduction in operating expenses during the first 10-15 years of 
ownership.

SECTION 9

RISK FACTORS & DUE DILIGENCE

PROCEDURES

DEVELOPMENT & CONSTRUCTION RISKS

While 74 Amity Street offers significant upside, any renovation of a 113-year-old shell 
building carries inherent risks that must be mitigated through professional project 
management.

KEY CONSIDERATIONS

• 
Structural Integrity: Although the exterior masonry appears sound, a full 
structural engineering survey is required to confirm the load-bearing capacity of 
existing joists for modern luxury finishes (e.g., stone countertops, heavy HVAC 
units).

• 
Environmental Remediation: Given the building’s age, prospective buyers 
should budget for the abatement of lead-based paint and potential asbestos-
containing materials (ACM) standard in buildings of this era.

• 
Subsurface Conditions: Any expansion or heavy mechanical installation must 
account for the high water table typical of properties located in proximity to the 
Brooklyn waterfront.

REGULATORY & ZONING HURDLES

The property’s value is tied to its status within a Limited Height District (LH-1) and its 
legal non-conforming use.

LANDMARKS & ZONING RISK

• 
LPC Approval: Because the property is located within a historic district, any 
changes to the exterior (windows, cornices, entryways) must be approved by the 
Landmarks Preservation Commission. This can impact the construction timeline.

• 
Certificates of Occupancy: The building is currently a "shell." Converting it to 
residential use will require obtaining a new Temporary Certificate of Occupancy 
(TCO) and eventually a Final CO.

• 
Department of Buildings (DOB): The "pre-existing" square footage advantage 
must be carefully protected during the filing process to ensure the 5,431 SF 
remains grandfathered.

and market cycles.

Risk Factor 
Mitigation Strategy

ratios.

Construction Cost 
Overruns

Include a minimum 15% hard-cost contingency in the 
development budget.

significantly lower at $32/SF.

Absorption Speed 
High demand in Cobble Hill suggests a "pre-leasing" 
strategy is viable.

DUE DILIGENCE CHECKLIST FOR PROSPECTIVE 
BUYERS

To assist in a smooth closing, the Seller has prepared a "Virtual Data Room." 
Prospective purchasers are encouraged to review the following:

1. Title Report: Confirming the absence of liens or historical encumbrances. 
2. Phase I Environmental Site Assessment (ESA): Reviewing historical uses of

the site.

3. Survey & ALTA Map: Confirming the 25’ x 75’ lot boundaries. 
4. Utility Letters: Confirming capacity for increased electrical and water loads. 
5. Property Tax Records: Verification of the current $27,261 assessment.
